topic: CMB Axis of Evil — post-EXP-295 c5 change-detected verification (verdict change Misleading → Self-Contradicted)
change_summary: drift-audit.json (generated 2026-05-12T05:15Z) flagged WIN-048 as priority_bucket=verdict_changed (Misleading → Self-Contradicted). Length fields are unchanged (claim 16, finding 149, detail_evidence 3234, detail_verdict_text 1532 — identical to c4 fingerprint); the verdict label is the only schema-comparable drift. New tldr_evidence (319) and tldr_verdict (239) fields are post-c4 schema growth. c4 fingerprint is_pre_tldr_schema=true, so tldr-bucket comparison is N/A; treated as legacy-field-stable + verdict-changed candidate. Sourced from drift-audit.json. obe_skipped=1 (WIN-047 already re-reviewed since audit ran).
our_argument_summary: WIN-048 now sits at verdict Self-Contradicted with finding 'False dilemma (ΛCDM problem ≠ dome evidence); anomaly significance eroded by Planck reanalysis; copper firmament (WIN-034) would block CMB microwaves'. The SC anchor is the WIN-034/copper-firmament dilemma already articulated verbatim in detail_evidence (both defender branches close: transparent-firmament contradicts WIN-034; thermal-source produces featureless 2.725 K blackbody with none of the observed acoustic peaks, E-mode polarization, or lensing). EXP-295 patches landed: verdict flipped, tldr_verdict was rewritten per c4 hole #3 recommendation (the 'verdict now matches' editor-process clause was excised in favor of the substantive copper-skin-depth anchor), and sub_rationale field was added marked '[audit-trail only — not rendered]'. EXP-295's optional hybrid TLDR (c4 hole #4, false-dilemma + Planck-erosion preservation) and WIN-034-Unfalsifiable preemption (c4 hole #1 option a) were NOT applied. The verdict change itself is sound on pattern (2) grounds.

relevant_files: raw-text/monitor.py (not re-examined this cycle — tags carried forward from c2/c3 validation, confirmed unchanged in c4)
dome_code_analysis.note: c5 is a change-detected post-patch verification of a verdict-label change. No code-tag re-validation required; the verdict change does not turn on any code-tag claim. Tags below carry forward unchanged.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Carried forward — no monitor.py domain tracks CMB Axis of Evil; only static live-power dashboard entry (Predicted: >2σ, Observed: 2.5σ, Tolerance: N/A). c4 detail_verdict_text documents this verbatim; current detail_verdict_text unchanged.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Carried forward — no standard-physics 'Axis of Evil' explanation is being renamed. Standard cosmology treats the alignment as a low-multipole low-significance anomaly; the dome adopts the anomaly's existence as confirmation without offering a different value.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Carried forward — Land & Magueijo 2005 (PRL 95, 071301) and Planck PR3/PR4 maps were public well before the dome monitor commit. detail_verdict_text still cites these.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Carried forward — no derivation chain from disc_radius / firmament_height / sun_altitude / moon_altitude to any CMB observable. detail_evidence still notes 'no analog in dome geometry'.

holes_found.description: STALE detail_extra conclusion contradicts current verdict label. The detail_extra block (dated 'Update 2026-04-10') concludes with the verbatim sentence: "Net effect on verdict: 'Misleading' still applies. The weakened claim is less misleading than the original, and we credit the author for the correction, but the structural problems ... are independent of the σ-value and remain fatal." This sentence was written when WIN-048's verdict was 'Misleading'. The verdict was reclassified to 'Self-Contradicted' on 2026-05-09 by EXP-295 — but the detail_extra block was not updated. A reader who opens WIN-048's expanded panel will see the verdict badge labeled 'Self-Contradicted' and then read in the same panel that 'Misleading still applies' — a direct reader-facing contradiction with our own verdict label. Confirmed by inspection of current data/wins.json:WIN-048:detail_extra (grep 'Misleading still applies' returns the line verbatim; verdict field shows 'Self-Contradicted'). This is exactly the kind of cross-page consistency failure we criticize the dome model for. A dome defender can legitimately weaponize this: 'Your own audit-trail says Misleading still applies; pick one.'
holes_found.recommendation: Decider should rewrite the detail_extra closing paragraph to reflect the current Self-Contradicted verdict. Suggested replacement for the final paragraph: 'Net effect on verdict: the WIN is Self-Contradicted (escalated from Misleading 2026-05-09 per EXP-295). The weakened statistical claim is less misleading than the original and we credit the author for the correction, but the structural problems — copper-firmament opacity to the 160 GHz CMB (WIN-034 self-contradiction), false-dilemma framing of ΛCDM anomalies as dome evidence, and no dome mechanism for CMB generation in flat geometry — operate at a different level from the σ-value. Lowering the statistical significance does not change the opacity of copper or the absence of a dome cosmology, so the structural verdict stands independent of how the Planck reanalysis lands.' This preserves the kernel-of-truth credit to the dome author and the existing prose's three-reasons structure while bringing the conclusion in line with the current verdict label.

holes_found.description: WIN-034-Unfalsifiable load-bearing tension still un-preempted in WIN-048 prose (carry-over from c4 hole #1, option a). The c4 review identified this latent tension: WIN-034's own verdict is 'Unfalsifiable' (verified: current data/wins.json:WIN-034:verdict = 'Unfalsifiable'), yet the SC verdict for WIN-048 hinges on treating WIN-034's cast-copper firmament as a load-bearing falsifying premise. c4 hole #1 offered two options: (a) a one-line clarification in WIN-048's prose noting that 'Unfalsifiable on direct material-sampling grounds does not exempt a published dome commitment from load-bearing-premise status for SC pattern (2) reasoning'; (b) re-examine WIN-034 separately. Option (b) is tracked by ISS-1921 (assigned-analyst, class_hint=holistic). Option (a) was the cheap fix that 'unblocks the EXP-295 verdict change' but was NOT applied during EXP-295 integration. A dome defender's strongest available rebuttal to the SC reading is: 'your review classifies WIN-034 as Unfalsifiable; you cannot weaponize an Unfalsifiable claim as a falsifying premise — that is self-stultifying.' The existing WIN-048 prose pre-rebuts the metaphorical defense via the WIN-029 Schumann chain, but does not pre-rebut the more abstract 'using-Unfalsifiable-as-premise' objection. Until ISS-1921 is resolved (which may take weeks if WIN-034 is reclassified, or longer if not), the tension remains in the live text.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider has three options: (i) apply the c4 hole #1 option (a) one-line clarification now as a cheap interim fix; the analyst's eventual WIN-034 EXP can then either supersede the clarification (if WIN-034 is reclassified) or fold it in (if WIN-034 stays Unfalsifiable). Suggested insertion in detail_evidence after the WIN-029 Schumann pre-rebuttal: 'Our review classifies WIN-034 itself as Unfalsifiable on direct material-sampling grounds (we cannot sample the alleged dome material). That classification does not exempt a published dome commitment from load-bearing-premise status for SC pattern (2) reasoning — the dome publishes cast-copper firmament as a physical specification and invokes it as Schumann conductive ceiling; we are asking whether the dome can coherently hold both that commitment and the CMB-observability premise of WIN-048.' (ii) Wait for ISS-1921 to land and rely on the analyst's full treatment. (iii) Re-route ISS-1921 with higher urgency. The c4 review explicitly recommended option (i) as the cheap unblocker — that recommendation was not picked up. Re-raising here so the carry-over is visible to the next decider pass.
holes_found.iss_link: ISS-1921 (covers option b — separate WIN-034 reclassification; option a remains unaddressed)

holes_found.description: WIN-048's sub_rationale field still references 'See Part 6.13' but Part 6.13 was never integrated into data/sections.json. Verified by grep across all parts (terms searched: '6.13', 'category-error import', 'MOND grouping', 'p6-613-mond' — zero hits in part1-part10 plus part1b/part2b/part8b). The proposed_subsection_html in monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-295-mond-grouping-verdict-review.json was drafted but never landed in part6.html. The sub_rationale field carries '[audit-trail only — not rendered]' so end-users are not exposed to the broken reference, but the internal audit-trail metadata now points to a non-existent target. ISS-1922 (assigned-analyst) tracks the broader sub_rationale-rendering / Part 6.13-prose question. ISS-2002 (assigned-analyst) tracks the same dangling reference for WIN-047. WIN-048's instance is functionally identical. Also noted (out-of-scope for this WIN but worth flagging to the decider): WIN-055 has NO sub_rationale field at all, despite being part of the same five-WIN MOND-grouping reclassification package — audit-trail completeness inconsistency across the EXP-295 batch.
holes_found.recommendation: No new issue needed for WIN-048 — fold into ISS-1922 resolution. When the analyst's EXP for ISS-1922 lands (either Part 6.13 prose integration or downgrade of the cross-reference), apply the resolution uniformly to WIN-047, WIN-048, WIN-052, WIN-054, and either backfill or omit WIN-055's sub_rationale to match the chosen pattern. If the analyst decides the proposed_subsection_html in EXP-295-mond-grouping-verdict-review.json is still wanted, the integration is straightforward — the draft is complete and already approved by the analyst. If not, the five sub_rationale fields should be rewritten to reference monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-295-mond-grouping-verdict-review.json directly (the actual audit-trail target).
holes_found.iss_link: ISS-1922 (sub_rationale rendering / Part 6.13 prose); ISS-2002 (analogous carry-over note for WIN-047)

unresolved_prior_cycle.note: c4 hole #1 was a two-option recommendation; option (b) is tracked by ISS-1921; option (a) — the cheap one-line clarification — was never applied and is not tracked separately. The current c5 hole #2 re-raises option (a) as a discrete cheap fix the decider can apply now without waiting for the analyst's WIN-034 reclassification proposal.

c4 hole #5 (sub_rationale rendering + Part 6.13 reference dangling) is tracked by ISS-1922; c5 hole #3 verifies the situation is unchanged on the live data (Part 6.13 still absent, sub_rationale still '[audit-trail only — not rendered]').

stronger_arguments.description: The c4 stronger_argument about WIN-034 SC reclassification (ISS-1921) gains an additional dimension here. WIN-034's own detail_evidence already states 'copper's skin depth is ~0.066 mm at 1 GHz, meaning even a paper-thin copper shell is opaque to all radio frequencies' and lists VLA/ALMA/FAST radio-astronomy observations as a contradicting class. Cross-WIN consistency check (Lens B) reveals a separate numerical issue in WIN-034 itself: 0.066 mm is the correct skin depth at 1 MHz, not 1 GHz. At 1 GHz copper skin depth is ~2 μm (= 0.002 mm), not 0.066 mm. The qualitative point (copper opaque to all radio) is correct, but a defender who checks the number will catch the frequency-vs-depth swap. This is a WIN-034 hole, not a WIN-048 hole — but it intersects with WIN-048's argument because WIN-048 cites 'copper skin depth at 160 GHz is ~0.2 μm' (correct: 0.163 μm) and a careful reader might cross-check WIN-034 and find the discrepancy. Recommend the decider either (a) open a WIN-034 issue for the 1 GHz/1 MHz fix, OR (b) fold the fix into whatever WIN-034 EXP eventually resolves ISS-1921.
sources: data/wins.json:WIN-034:detail_evidence — '~0.066 mm at 1 GHz' (frequency/depth swap; correct at 1 MHz)
data/wins.json:WIN-048:detail_evidence — '~0.2 μm at 160 GHz' (correct; computed 0.163 μm)
Quantitative verification in this review's quantitative_verification field

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome author identified a real cosmological-research topic in the CMB low-multipole alignments (data/wins.json:WIN-048:detail_evidence — cites Planck 2015 Results XXIII and Land & Magueijo 2005). Engagement with the literature is correct, not strawmanned. More importantly, the dome author voluntarily downgraded the WIN-048 confidence claim on 2026-03-23 from '>99.9% confirmed by two missions' to '2–3σ' after the Planck full-mission reanalysis (data/wins.json:WIN-048:detail_extra Update block). That is a responsible self-correction the dome author was not forced to make.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The kernel-of-truth (the anomaly is a real research topic; the author corrected the confidence claim honestly) operates at the statistical level. The Self-Contradicted verdict operates at the structural level: the dome's own cast-copper firmament commitment (data/wins.json:WIN-034) precludes 160 GHz microwave transmission at any reasonable thickness (skin depth 0.163 μm verified — see this review's quantitative_verification), which forecloses the dome's ability to observe the CMB at all. The thermal-emission defender branch produces a featureless blackbody with none of the observed acoustic peaks, E-mode polarization, or lensing signal. Both defender branches close on the dome's own commitments. The statistical-significance correction does not interact with the structural-foreclosure argument, so the SC verdict is independent of where the Planck reanalysis lands. The dome's intellectual honesty about σ-values is genuinely creditable and the live detail_extra continues to credit it — the c5 finding is only that the closing 'Misleading still applies' line is now stale relative to the verdict label, not that the credit itself is misplaced.

deeper_analysis: EXP-295's verdict change is sound on the pattern (2) SC anchor (the WIN-034 copper-firmament dilemma already articulated verbatim in detail_evidence with both defender branches closing). The c5 change-detected pass focuses on what landed vs what was deferred. Landed: verdict flip, tldr_verdict rewrite per c4 hole #3, sub_rationale field with audit-trail marker per c4 hole #5 (partial fix — marker added but Part 6.13 reference not downgraded). Deferred: c4 hole #1 option (a) one-line clarification (carry-over to c5 hole #2); the false-dilemma+Planck hybrid TLDR per c4 hole #4 (defensible — current tldr_verdict concentrates load-bearing weight on the WIN-034 anchor, acceptable for an SC TLDR). The freshly-detected major issue is the stale detail_extra conclusion ('Misleading still applies'), which the c4 review did not flag because the verdict change had not landed yet — that block was last updated 2026-04-10 and was visually contiguous with the now-stale verdict label. This is exactly the kind of timestamp-drift inconsistency a holistic verdict-taxonomy audit would catch; PROP-022's verdict-tally tests should also catch any prose count that hardcodes Misleading-15 vs Self-Contradicted-16 (verified separately: build.js / test.js do not appear to hardcode WIN-048's verdict label, so the test suite alone wouldn't catch this in-text drift). Net: verdict label is correct; the prose around the verdict needs a quick patch.

advocate_mode.best_defense: Imagine a dome defender who has read both our review's six methodological principles AND our current WIN-048 panel. Their strongest single move is to weaponize our own audit-trail against us: 'You upgraded WIN-048 to Self-Contradicted on 2026-05-09. Your own detail_extra block — visible on the same page, dated 2026-04-10 but never revised — concludes "Net effect on verdict: Misleading still applies." Pick one. If Misleading still applies (per your audit-trail), the SC label is editorial overreach. If SC applies (per your verdict badge), your audit-trail is lying about its own conclusion. Either way your review is internally inconsistent on its own published surface — which is the exact failure mode you spend Part 2 cataloguing in the dome model.' This is a tu quoque move but it lands cleanly because the inconsistency is verbatim on our page. A secondary move (weaker, more abstract): 'You classify WIN-034 as Unfalsifiable but use WIN-034 as a falsifying premise for WIN-048 SC. You cannot have it both ways: either WIN-034 is a published falsifiable dome commitment (in which case downgrade it from Unfalsifiable) or it is not (in which case WIN-048 SC has no premise).' Both defenses point at the same architectural seam: our review's verdict labels have drifted faster than the prose that supports them.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: The primary defense (stale detail_extra) is fully closed by patching hole #1 above — rewrite the closing paragraph to land on the SC verdict explicitly. Cost: one paragraph edit. The secondary defense (WIN-034 Unfalsifiable as premise) is partially closed by applying c4 hole #1 option (a) clarification per c5 hole #2, and fully closed when ISS-1921 lands. Recommend the decider apply BOTH hole #1 and hole #2 patches in the same decider run — they address the same architectural seam and the cost is small.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: WIN-034's detail_evidence has a unit-frequency error in its skin-depth example ('~0.066 mm at 1 GHz' should be '~2 μm at 1 GHz' or '~0.066 mm at 1 MHz' — see this review's stronger_arguments). The qualitative claim (copper opaque to radio) holds; the numerical example does not. This is a WIN-034 hole, not a WIN-048 hole — but it intersects because both WINs invoke the copper-skin-depth argument and the numbers should be self-consistent across the pair. WIN-048's '~0.2 μm at 160 GHz' is correct (computed 0.163 μm). WIN-029, WIN-053, WIN-067 are SC pattern (2) precedents and are internally compatible with WIN-048's pattern (2) reading.
cross_win_consistency.cross_win_finding_routed_to: stronger_arguments (WIN-034 numeric fix, not a WIN-048 hole)

claims_checked: WIN-048 detail_evidence: 'copper skin depth at 160 GHz is ~0.2 μm'
WIN-048 tldr_verdict: 'copper at 160 GHz has a skin depth of ~0.2 micrometers'
Cross-WIN: WIN-034 detail_evidence: 'copper's skin depth is ~0.066 mm at 1 GHz'
quantitative_verification.computations: Skin depth δ = 1/√(πfμ₀σ) with σ_Cu = 5.96×10⁷ S/m and μ = μ₀. At f = 160 GHz: δ = 0.163 μm. At f = 1 GHz: δ = 2.06 μm = 0.00206 mm. At f = 1 MHz: δ = 65.2 μm = 0.0652 mm. The '~0.066 mm' value WIN-034 attributes to 1 GHz is actually correct for 1 MHz, off by a factor of ~31.6 (= √1000) in frequency.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: WIN-048's two skin-depth claims (~0.2 μm at 160 GHz in detail_evidence and tldr_verdict) are VERIFIED — actual computed value 0.163 μm, rounds to ~0.2 μm. WIN-034's skin-depth example ('~0.066 mm at 1 GHz') is INCORRECT — 0.066 mm is the value at 1 MHz, not 1 GHz. The qualitative point (copper opaque to radio frequencies) is unaffected. Routed to stronger_arguments as a WIN-034 fix, not a WIN-048 fix.

package_summary_for_decider: Change-detected c5 review of WIN-048 post-EXP-295. Verdict change Misleading→Self-Contradicted is SOUND on the pattern (2) SC anchor — the WIN-034 copper-firmament dilemma with both defender branches closing. THREE holes found: (1) MAJOR — stale detail_extra block still concludes 'Net effect on verdict: Misleading still applies'; this is a reader-facing contradiction with the current SC verdict label and is the most quotable single thing a dome defender can extract from the page. Cheap fix: rewrite the closing paragraph. (2) MODERATE — c4 hole #1 option (a) cheap one-line preemption of the WIN-034-Unfalsifiable-as-falsifying-premise objection was never applied; ISS-1921 (assigned-analyst) covers option (b) but not option (a); carry-over from c4. Cheap fix available. (3) MINOR — sub_rationale's 'See Part 6.13' reference is dangling because Part 6.13 was never integrated into part6.html; tracked by ISS-1922; not reader-visible because field carries '[audit-trail only — not rendered]'. ALSO NOTED out-of-scope: WIN-055 has no sub_rationale at all, audit-trail inconsistency across the EXP-295 batch (decider may want a quick backfill or omission to make the batch uniform); WIN-034 has an internal unit-frequency error in its 1 GHz skin-depth example (this is a WIN-034 issue, not a WIN-048 issue; recommend the decider either open a WIN-034 fix or fold into ISS-1921's eventual EXP). Quantitative verification: WIN-048's '~0.2 μm at 160 GHz' skin depth is verified (computed 0.163 μm). Advocate-mode rating 4 — the stale detail_extra is a 'pick one' attack that lands cleanly on our own published surface; closing hole #1 retires that attack. Net: verdict change is correct; one major patch (hole #1) + one moderate patch (hole #2) recommended in the same decider run.
